How to find ideas for a topical news story email
- Target big news stories in the media that you can tie in (these have
to be in media that your customer pays attention to)
- North Korea talking about nuking the US (sell survival food)
- Cuts to social security
- Game Changers documentary (all about plant based, could use
this to promote plant-based or go the other way and promote
keto/paleo)
- 2020 election
- 5g internet (and controversies around it) gaining steam
- Bezos/Buffet and billionaire’s tax
-
- Target people or things that your customer HATES
- AOC, Hillary Clinton, Elizabeth Warren
- celebrity saying something absurd that your customer would hate
- Target people or things that you customer LOVES
- Trump
- celebrity weight loss/health stuff
- celebrity on “Doomsday preppers” talking about the coming
apocalypse
- Warnings/Alerts from agencies/organizations/people they give
credibility to
- FDA issues dire warning about CBD oil
- Harvard doctor discovers “alzheimer’s reversing cocktail”
- Social security running out of funding
- FEMA statement about “being prepared”
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 13 --

Lead with the topical news story in the email, and then
transition to what you’re selling.
Big tip - you can usually keep the selling part similar and
just rotate out new stories that are on people’s minds
----- Examples of topical emails ----
Subj: Trump’s new 5G plan is opening the flood gates
Palm Beach Daily Reader,
The Trump administration just unveiled a new 5G FAST plan...
Trump's 5G plan is opening the flood gates to 3 million new jobs, $12.3 trillion
in economic growth, and a brand new 5G device.

Sincerely,
Van Bryan

Subj: think medical marijuana is “fake news”?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 13 --

Dear Stansberry Digest Reader,
Senior NBC News correspondent, Tom Brokaw, was diagnosed with cancer in 2013.
He suffered from multiple myeloma, a rare form of cancer that creates tremendous pain in your
bones.
Brokaw was able to get treatment at the Mayo Clinic.
However, he was shocked at the cost of the care he received...
"I counted up the price and it was something like $10,000 a day, you know, that I was spending
on drugs."
Even still, the disease is fatal... it is only a matter of time.
Today, Brokaw still suffers from pain in his bones, and specifically his back.
But he's turned away from traditional pain killers and recently announced he is using medical
cannabis to treat his pain.
Brokaw's story is a more famous example of one we're hearing from folks of all walks of life.
Republicans... democrats... the rich... and the poor...
Fact is, for many people, cannabis is about more than getting "high."
Yes, many people do use it recreationally. But we've read enough opinions from serious medical
professionals to know that medical marijuana is far from "fake news."
And in a country where the conversation about rising medical costs is becoming a central focus
of policy and debate...
It is clear to us that lower cost alternative forms of medicine, like medical cannabis, are only
going to grow in popularity – both in Washington and on Main Street.
Thomas Carroll, the newest Senior Analyst to join our team, agrees.
Tom spent 20 years as a healthcare analyst on Wall Street.
And he says that he sees a new wave of interest coming to the cannabis industry.
It has the potential to help a lot of people in our country.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 13 --

And according to Tom, it has the potential to help you financially too, if you understand what
this industry will look like a few years down the road... instead of focusing on what it looks like
today.
He recently sat down in our studio to share his unique perspective on the industry and where it is
heading.
To see a recap we put together (which you can skim in just a few minutes), click here.
Regards,
Justin Brill
Editor, The Stansberry Digest
